title: LLM Operations Engineer
description:
Job Summary
A global leader in technology consulting and digital transformation, the client delivers innovative solutions that enhance business performance and resilience. With deep expertise in cloud computing, data analytics, and AI-driven insights, they help organizations modernize systems, improve scalability, and accelerate operational efficiency. Their end-to-end approach to enterprise transformation empowers companies across industries to innovate with confidence and stay competitive in a rapidly evolving digital landscape. Our client is looking for a LLM Operations Engineering to maintain and optimize databases and scripts to improve operational efficiency.

Main Responsibilities

  • Track activities run concurrently on daily basis and each track needs to work with multiple parties including Subject Matter Expert, middleware, the interfacing counter-part and other suppliers, to ensure end-to-end operability of the interfaces.
  • Manage all incoming and outgoing system interfaces from OSIT/WOG systems. This includes external interfaces, external projects, eServices, and applications from MINDEF/SAF on Internet portals and Commercial Cloud.
  • Provide daily operations support in terms of batch job monitoring and error resolution.
  • Provide inputs for the development and deployment of CRs related to interfaces, such as during requirements gathering and UAT.
  • Profile and analyse data for issues and discrepancies using software tools (such as Microsoft office tools, SAP programs/queries or SQL provided by Authority) when required.
  • Provide analysis and consultation services to OSIT/WOG systems to address operational issues and advise on resolutions to data-related issues.
  • Coordinate with the data owner, for HR data where man-in-the-loop decision is required.
  • Monitor error logs and ensure timely resolution of interface errors.
  • Coordinate and perform secretarial role for meetings with different stakeholders and internal meetings.
  • Generate reports to management, various forums and prepare slides for presentations during meetings when required.
  • Maintain project resource assignments, track status and initiate appropriate actions in response to deviations from plan to keep the project on-track.
  • Perform data uploads for manual data transfers where required by Authority.
  • Review, propose and update changes to the documentation where necessary.

Qualification and Profile

  • At least 2 years of working experience in information technology.
  • Experience in SAP HCM ERP is preferred.
  • Experience in operation support including end user support, troubleshooting and testing is preferred.
  • SQL working experience and competent in SQL Programming is preferred.
  • Experience in MINDEF/SAF HR projects is preferred.


Next Step

  • If interested, you can click on “Apply here” or write an e-mail to dimple.jain@adecco.com with your updated resume.
  • NOTE: - Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted back.

Dimple Jain
EA LIcence No. 91C2918
Personnel Registration No. R22107270

postedCompany.description:
The Adecco Group is the world's leading provider of HR solutions. With over 35,000 FTE employees and over 5,200 branches, in over 60 countries and territories around the world, we offer a wide variety of services, connecting more than 650,000 associates with over 100,000 clients every day.

The services we offer fall into the broad categories of temporary staffing, permanent placement, career transition and talent development, as well as outsourcing and consulting. The Adecco Group is based in Zurich-Glattbrugg, Switzerland and we are a Fortune Global 500 company listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange.
postedCompany.name: ADECCO PERSONNEL PTE LTD
